addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: add-privileged-ssh-tabtarget (₢A-AAw) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  Code amendment with curia-side verification.
  
  ## Docket
  
  Add `buw-jpS` privileged-SSH tabtarget — thin admin-side pipe that
  runs an arbitrary command as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` on a BURN node.
  Channel: param1 = investiture; remaining args joined as the remote
  command. Pass-through — no shell wrapping; operator prepends
  `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as needed for the platform.
  
  Scope: any BURN that has a local BURP profile (Linux/Mac/Windows
  alike). Linux/Mac admin trust comes from operator-managed
  `ssh-copy-id`; Windows admin trust comes from fenestrate. The
  tabtarget itself does not assert platform — the privileged-key auth
  path handles success/failure uniformly.
  
  Drop "interactive if no extra args" from the earlier sketch — require
  ≥1 command arg; die with a usage diagnostic on missing command.
  Interactive variant deferred to real need.
  
  Code shape:
  
  - New public function `bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND...` in
    `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Uses `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
    `IdentitiesOnly=yes`, `BatchMode=yes`, key-only auth — no password
    fallback. No shell wrapping.
  - New CLI command in `bujb_cli.sh` with appropriate furnish (BURC +
    BURN + BURP regimes kindled, `bujb_resolve_investiture` called for
    privkey validation per existing pattern).
  - New tabtarget `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh` enrolled in
    `buz_zipper.sh`. Channel: param1.
  
  BUS0 update:
  
  - New §BUSJPS section parallel to BUSJPF, describing the privileged-
    SSH verb with: any-BURN scope, param1+args channel, pass-through
    shell semantics, no persistent state change (ephemeral exec).
  - Mapping section gains the new tabtarget entry.
  
  Downstream consumer (next pace, AAv): pre-flight diagnostic emits
  copy-paste hints in the form `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'wsl --install
  …'`. AAv depends on this pace landing first.
  
  Done when:
  
  - `bash -n` green on `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green
  - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green at baseline (no regression)
  - BUSJPS section present in BUS0; mapping section updated
  - Curia-side smoke: `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'whoami'` returns the
    expected privileged user name on at least one platform reachable
    from the test station

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
8	WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
9	integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
10	WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.
11	
12	## Current Concept
13	
14	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
15	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
16	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing both verbs). Neither
17	the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in
18	BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
19	
20	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
21	
22	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
23	  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
24	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
25	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
26	  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
27	  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
28	  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
29	  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
30	  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.
31	
32	**BURC addition:**
33	
34	- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
35	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
36	
37	**Two verbs (Fenestrate, Garrison):**
38	
39	- **Fenestrate** — Windows-OpenSSH-only `sshd_config` harden plus admin SSH
40	  key-trust establishment. Uniform across shell-letters (no variant).
41	  Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` killing its own
42	  session. Tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config application).
43	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per-shell-letter
44	  (b/c/w). Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
45	  authorized_keys. First action is admin SSH via key auth; failure surfaces
46	  immediately.
47	- For Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb — admin trust is operator-manual
48	  (e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); garrison runs against an existing key-trusted admin
49	  foothold. For Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening on Windows nodes (in addition to
50	  Windows OpenSSH), hardening is operator-manual.
51	
52	**Garrison-destructive model:**
53	
54	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
55	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
56	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
57	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
58	- Admin-side sshd configuration is fenestrate's scope on Windows;
59	  operator-manual elsewhere.
60	
61	**Cross-cutting premises:**
62	
63	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
64	  it by reference, never duplicates.
65	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
66	  material; operator owns all key administration.
67	
68	**Tabtarget colophons (14 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
69	
70	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
71	- `buw-jpF` — Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform) — 1
72	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
73	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
74	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
75	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
76	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
77	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1
78	
79	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
80	
81	BCG-compliant module housing both fenestrate and garrison implementations,
82	hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings (b/c/w)
83	used by garrison on the workload account, the workload privkey destination
84	path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name
85	(`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive
86	set used by fenestrate. SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries
87	no shell selection.
88	
89	**Fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform):**
90	
91	Two-phase remote-run because `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own
92	ssh session.
93	
94	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
95	
96	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
97	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
98	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
99	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Fenestrate
100	   process never sees the password.
101	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
102	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
103	   (no `command=`; fenestrate is uniform). Performed before any sshd_config
104	   change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
105	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
106	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
107	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
108	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
109	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
110	   values. Keeps PowerShell out of parsing (its error semantics are
111	   unreliable).
112	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
113	   malformed config aborts fenestrate before it could brick running sshd).
114	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
115	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
116	   expected phase boundary).
117	
118	Phase 2 (key auth only):
119	
120	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
121	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
122	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
123	   a session.
124	9. Verify post-state: the admin session authenticates by key alone and the
125	   running sshd serves the hardened configuration.
126	
127	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
128	
129	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
130	   only (no password fallback). On Windows, presupposes fenestrate has run
131	   for this investiture. On Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-managed
132	   (e.g., `ssh-copy-id` placed the admin pubkey).
133	2. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
134	   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
135	   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
136	3. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
137	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
138	4. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
139	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
140	5. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
141	   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
142	   outbound to GitHub).
143	6. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
144	
145	## Heat Sequence
146	
147	**Spec foundation:**
148	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
149	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
150	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb
151	
152	**Implementation:**
153	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
154	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 14 tabtargets, fenestrate + garrison
155	  ceremonies, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries
156	
157	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
158	- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
159	
160	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
161	- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
162	- Garrison-WSL exercise
163	- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
164	- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)
165	
166	## Deferred (named for future return)
167	
168	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
169	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
170	  only.
171	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
172	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
173	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
174	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
175	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
176	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
177	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
178	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
179	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
180	- **Cygwin / WSL sshd hardening verb** — fenestrate covers Windows OpenSSH
181	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
182	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
183	  fenestrate sibling.
184	- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
185	  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
186	  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
187	  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
188	  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
189	  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
190	  a time, never both running concurrently).
191	- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
192	  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.
193	
194	## Standing Notes
195	
196	- **Fenestrate handles first-time admin trust establishment on Windows.**
197	  On a fresh node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls
198	  through to a /dev/tty password prompt; operator types the Windows admin
199	  password once. Fenestrate then installs the admin pubkey, sets the icacls
200	  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
201	  and reconnects to verify by key. Subsequent runs use key auth
202	  automatically. The operator's manual Windows scope reduces to: install
203	  OpenSSH server, enable the service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes`
204	  temporarily, allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known
205	  password.
206	- **Linux/Mac admin trust is operator-managed.** No fenestrate verb;
207	  operator places admin pubkey via `ssh-copy-id` (or equivalent), and
208	  garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone.
209	- **Two-phase fenestrate is structural, not optional.** `Restart-Service
210	  sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any post-restart verification must
211	  happen over a fresh session. Bash orchestrator treats the post-restart
212	  disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
213	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
214	  prompts interactively; fenestrate and garrison would hang. Operator
215	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
216	  privkey-at-rest protection.
217	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
218	  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
219	  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
220	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
221	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
222	  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
223	- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
224	  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
225	  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
226	  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
227	  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
228	  daemon ever runs concurrently.
229	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
230	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
231	  path construction.
232	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
233	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
234	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
235	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
236	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
237	  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
238	
239	# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAw
240	
241	## Character
242	Code amendment with curia-side verification.
243	
244	## Docket
245	
246	Add `buw-jpS` privileged-SSH tabtarget — thin admin-side pipe that
247	runs an arbitrary command as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` on a BURN node.
248	Channel: param1 = investiture; remaining args joined as the remote
249	command. Pass-through — no shell wrapping; operator prepends
250	`powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as needed for the platform.
251	
252	Scope: any BURN that has a local BURP profile (Linux/Mac/Windows
253	alike). Linux/Mac admin trust comes from operator-managed
254	`ssh-copy-id`; Windows admin trust comes from fenestrate. The
255	tabtarget itself does not assert platform — the privileged-key auth
256	path handles success/failure uniformly.
257	
258	Drop "interactive if no extra args" from the earlier sketch — require
259	≥1 command arg; die with a usage diagnostic on missing command.
260	Interactive variant deferred to real need.
261	
262	Code shape:
263	
264	- New public function `bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND...` in
265	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Uses `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
266	  `IdentitiesOnly=yes`, `BatchMode=yes`, key-only auth — no password
267	  fallback. No shell wrapping.
268	- New CLI command in `bujb_cli.sh` with appropriate furnish (BURC +
269	  BURN + BURP regimes kindled, `bujb_resolve_investiture` called for
270	  privkey validation per existing pattern).
271	- New tabtarget `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh` enrolled in
272	  `buz_zipper.sh`. Channel: param1.
273	
274	BUS0 update:
275	
276	- New §BUSJPS section parallel to BUSJPF, describing the privileged-
277	  SSH verb with: any-BURN scope, param1+args channel, pass-through
278	  shell semantics, no persistent state change (ephemeral exec).
279	- Mapping section gains the new tabtarget entry.
280	
281	Downstream consumer (next pace, AAv): pre-flight diagnostic emits
282	copy-paste hints in the form `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'wsl --install
283	…'`. AAv depends on this pace landing first.
284	
285	Done when:
286	
287	- `bash -n` green on `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`
288	- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green
289	- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green at baseline (no regression)
290	- BUSJPS section present in BUS0; mapping section updated
291	- Curia-side smoke: `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'whoami'` returns the
292	  expected privileged user name on at least one platform reachable
293	  from the test station
294

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing both verbs). Neither
the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in
BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.

**BURC addition:**

- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.

**Two verbs (Fenestrate, Garrison):**

- **Fenestrate** — Windows-OpenSSH-only `sshd_config` harden plus admin SSH
  key-trust establishment. Uniform across shell-letters (no variant).
  Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` killing its own
  session. Tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config application).
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per-shell-letter
  (b/c/w). Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys. First action is admin SSH via key auth; failure surfaces
  immediately.
- For Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb — admin trust is operator-manual
  (e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); garrison runs against an existing key-trusted admin
  foothold. For Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening on Windows nodes (in addition to
  Windows OpenSSH), hardening is operator-manual.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side sshd configuration is fenestrate's scope on Windows;
  operator-manual elsewhere.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (14 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpF` — Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform) — 1
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing both fenestrate and garrison implementations,
hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings (b/c/w)
used by garrison on the workload account, the workload privkey destination
path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name
(`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive
set used by fenestrate. SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries
no shell selection.

**Fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform):**

Two-phase remote-run because `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own
ssh session.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Fenestrate
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; fenestrate is uniform). Performed before any sshd_config
   change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values. Keeps PowerShell out of parsing (its error semantics are
   unreliable).
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts fenestrate before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-state: the admin session authenticates by key alone and the
   running sshd serves the hardened configuration.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback). On Windows, presupposes fenestrate has run
   for this investiture. On Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-managed
   (e.g., `ssh-copy-id` placed the admin pubkey).
2. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
3. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
4. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
5. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
   outbound to GitHub).
6. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 14 tabtargets, fenestrate + garrison
  ceremonies, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
- Garrison-WSL exercise
- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL sshd hardening verb** — fenestrate covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  fenestrate sibling.
- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
  a time, never both running concurrently).
- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.

## Standing Notes

- **Fenestrate handles first-time admin trust establishment on Windows.**
  On a fresh node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls
  through to a /dev/tty password prompt; operator types the Windows admin
  password once. Fenestrate then installs the admin pubkey, sets the icacls
  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
  and reconnects to verify by key. Subsequent runs use key auth
  automatically. The operator's manual Windows scope reduces to: install
  OpenSSH server, enable the service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes`
  temporarily, allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known
  password.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust is operator-managed.** No fenestrate verb;
  operator places admin pubkey via `ssh-copy-id` (or equivalent), and
  garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone.
- **Two-phase fenestrate is structural, not optional.** `Restart-Service
  sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any post-restart verification must
  happen over a fresh session. Bash orchestrator treats the post-restart
  disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; fenestrate and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
  daemon ever runs concurrently.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
  path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAw

## Character
Code amendment with curia-side verification.

## Docket

Add `buw-jpS` privileged-SSH tabtarget — thin admin-side pipe that
runs an arbitrary command as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` on a BURN node.
Channel: param1 = investiture; remaining args joined as the remote
command. Pass-through — no shell wrapping; operator prepends
`powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as needed for the platform.

Scope: any BURN that has a local BURP profile (Linux/Mac/Windows
alike). Linux/Mac admin trust comes from operator-managed
`ssh-copy-id`; Windows admin trust comes from fenestrate. The
tabtarget itself does not assert platform — the privileged-key auth
path handles success/failure uniformly.

Drop "interactive if no extra args" from the earlier sketch — require
≥1 command arg; die with a usage diagnostic on missing command.
Interactive variant deferred to real need.

Code shape:

- New public function `bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND...` in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Uses `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
  `IdentitiesOnly=yes`, `BatchMode=yes`, key-only auth — no password
  fallback. No shell wrapping.
- New CLI command in `bujb_cli.sh` with appropriate furnish (BURC +
  BURN + BURP regimes kindled, `bujb_resolve_investiture` called for
  privkey validation per existing pattern).
- New tabtarget `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh` enrolled in
  `buz_zipper.sh`. Channel: param1.

BUS0 update:

- New §BUSJPS section parallel to BUSJPF, describing the privileged-
  SSH verb with: any-BURN scope, param1+args channel, pass-through
  shell semantics, no persistent state change (ephemeral exec).
- Mapping section gains the new tabtarget entry.

Downstream consumer (next pace, AAv): pre-flight diagnostic emits
copy-paste hints in the form `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'wsl --install
…'`. AAv depends on this pace landing first.

Done when:

- `bash -n` green on `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`, `bujb_cli.sh`
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green at baseline (no regression)
- BUSJPS section present in BUS0; mapping section updated
- Curia-side smoke: `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'whoami'` returns the
  expected privileged user name on at least one platform reachable
  from the test station

**Paddock shape:** Jurisdiction = BUK feature for reaching remote nodes deterministically. Two regimes (BURN node-shape, BURP credentials), two verbs (Fenestrate Windows-only sshd harden + admin trust; Garrison per-shell-letter workload provisioning). Operational seat: `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`. 14 colophons enrolled.

**Recent work:** Standing Notes paddock entry locking Docker Desktop as release-1 runtime on Windows; §Supported Platforms in README qualifying release-1 against Linux Docker, Windows Docker Desktop (WSL2), Mac Docker Desktop.

**Next pace:** ₢A-AAw **add-privileged-ssh-tabtarget** [rough] — thin admin-side pipe `buw-jpS` that runs an arbitrary command as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` on a BURN node. Pass-through, key-only auth, ≥1 command arg required. Downstream consumer (AAv) needs this for pre-flight copy-paste hints.

**Name fits docket** — no relabel.

**Approach (4 touches):**
1. Add `bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND...` to `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` — `IdentitiesOnly=yes`, `BatchMode=yes`, key-only, no shell wrapping
2. Add CLI command in `bujb_cli.sh` mirroring fenestrate/garrison furnish (BURC+BURN+BURP kindled, `bujb_resolve_investiture` for privkey validation)
3. Enroll `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh` in `buz_zipper.sh`, channel param1
4. New §BUSJPS in BUS0 parallel to BUSJPF + mapping section update

**Done gates:** `bash -n`, `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`, `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`, BUS0 sections, curia-side smoke `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> 'whoami'`.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — coupled cross-module change (function + CLI + zipper + spec); existing patterns to mirror but the BCG/furnish discipline rewards judgment.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
20	#
21	# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22	# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23	# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24	# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25	# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26	# hardening directive set.
27	#
28	# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation. A future
29	# PowerShell sibling would mint as bujp_jurisdiction.ps1.
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	# Multiple inclusion detection
34	test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
35	ZBUJB_SOURCED=1
36	
37	# Tinder constants (pure string literals — available at source time)
38	
39	# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
40	# Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
41	# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
42	# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
43	BUJB_command_b='command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
44	BUJB_command_c='command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
45	BUJB_command_w='command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
46	
47	# Shell-letter -> workload privkey destination path on the remote
48	# (relative to the workload account home directory).
49	BUJB_workload_keypath_b='.ssh/id_ed25519'
50	BUJB_workload_keypath_c='.ssh/id_ed25519'
51	BUJB_workload_keypath_w='.ssh/id_ed25519'
52	
53	# Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
54	BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
55	
56	# Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
57	# fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
58	# bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
59	BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
60	PasswordAuthentication no
61	PermitEmptyPasswords no'
62	
63	######################################################################
64	# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)
65	
66	zbujb_kindle() {
67	  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"
68	
69	  # Fenestrate temp file paths — ssh stdout/stderr captured here so
70	  # callers parse from disk (no `$(ssh ...)` capture).
71	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stdout.txt"
72	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stderr.txt"
73	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stdout.txt"
74	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stderr.txt"
75	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stdout.txt"
76	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stderr.txt"
77	
78	  # stat captures (per-key-slot for parallel forensics on resolve).
79	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stdout.txt"
80	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stderr.txt"
81	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stdout.txt"
82	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stderr.txt"
83	
84	  # ssh-keygen dry-load captures (resolve-time, per slot).
85	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stdout.txt"
86	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stderr.txt"
87	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stdout.txt"
88	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stderr.txt"
89	
90	  # ssh-keygen pubkey-emit captures (garrison step4 = workload pubkey,
91	  # fenestrate phase1 = admin pubkey).
92	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stdout.txt"
93	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stderr.txt"
94	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stdout.txt"
95	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stderr.txt"
96	
97	  # base64 capture (garrison step5 = workload privkey b64-encoded for
98	  # heredoc transport to remote).
99	  readonly ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_key_b64_stdout.txt"
100	  readonly ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_key_b64_stderr.txt"
101	
102	  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
103	}
104	
105	zbujb_sentinel() {
106	  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
107	}
108	
109	# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME SLOT -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
110	# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
111	# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label. SLOT
112	# (priv|work) selects the kindle temp-file pair used for stat + dry-load
113	# stdout/stderr capture so failures preserve forensic evidence per BCG.
114	zbujb_check_key_file() {
115	  local z_path="${1:-}"
116	  local z_varname="${2:-}"
117	  local z_slot="${3:-}"
118	
119	  test -n "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
120	  test -f "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"
121	
122	  local z_stat_stdout="" z_stat_stderr="" z_dry_stdout="" z_dry_stderr=""
123	  case "${z_slot}" in
124	    priv)
125	      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV}"
126	      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV}"
127	      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV}"
128	      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV}"
129	      ;;
130	    work)
131	      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK}"
132	      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK}"
133	      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK}"
134	      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK}"
135	      ;;
136	    *)
137	      buc_die "zbujb_check_key_file: invalid slot (expected priv|work): '${z_slot}'"
138	      ;;
139	  esac
140	
141	  # BSD stat first (Mac), GNU stat fallback (Linux). The failed flavor's
142	  # stderr is overwritten by the successful flavor's invocation; on total
143	  # failure z_stat_stderr holds the GNU-attempt error.
144	  if stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
145	    : # macOS / BSD stat succeeded
146	  elif stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
147	    : # GNU/Linux stat succeeded
148	  else
149	    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path} — see ${z_stat_stderr}"
150	  fi
151	
152	  local z_mode
153	  z_mode=$(<"${z_stat_stdout}")
154	  z_mode="${z_mode//$'\n'/}"
155	  test "${z_mode}" = "600" \
156	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"
157	
158	  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" \
159	      > "${z_dry_stdout}" \
160	      2> "${z_dry_stderr}" \
161	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path} — see ${z_dry_stderr}"
162	}
163	
164	######################################################################
165	# Public Functions (bujb_*)
166	
167	# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole runtime cross-validation entrypoint.
168	#
169	# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
170	# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
171	# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that the
172	# investiture name (BUZ_FOLIO) equals a registered investiture —
173	# enforcement is by file presence of the matching BURN profile).
174	#
175	# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
176	# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted) —
177	# the validation regime enforce cannot do because it requires running
178	# ssh-keygen against the private key. After validation, sets ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
179	# as the I-have-been-validated sentinel. Callers reference the regime vars
180	# directly (BURP_*, BURN_*, BURC_*); no separate published namespace.
181	# Single-call-per-process; subsequent calls die.
182	bujb_resolve_investiture() {
183	  zbujb_sentinel
184	  zburc_sentinel
185	  zburn_sentinel
186	  zburp_sentinel
187	
188	  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
189	    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"
190	
191	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE" priv
192	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"   work
193	
194	  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
195	}
196	
197	# bujb_command_for_capture SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
198	# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w. _capture form so callers
199	# may use `$()` per BCG line 502; returns 1 on invalid letter, callers must
200	# `|| buc_die`.
201	bujb_command_for_capture() {
202	  zbujb_sentinel
203	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
204	  case "${z_letter}" in
205	    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
206	    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
207	    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
208	    *) return 1 ;;
209	  esac
210	}
211	
212	# bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload privkey
213	# remote destination path (relative to workload home) for b, c, or w.
214	# Returns 1 on invalid letter; callers must `|| buc_die`.
215	bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture() {
216	  zbujb_sentinel
217	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
218	  case "${z_letter}" in
219	    b) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_b}" ;;
220	    c) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_c}" ;;
221	    w) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_w}" ;;
222	    *) return 1 ;;
223	  esac
224	}
225	
226	######################################################################
227	# Internal: Garrison helpers
228	
229	zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
230	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
231	  case "${z_letter}" in
232	    b|c|w) ;;
233	    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
234	  esac
235	}
236	
237	# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BURN_PLATFORM
238	# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
239	zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
240	  zbujb_sentinel
241	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
242	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
243	  case "${z_letter}" in
244	    b)
245	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
246	        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
247	        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
248	      esac
249	      ;;
250	    c|w)
251	      test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
252	        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
253	      ;;
254	  esac
255	}
256	
257	# zbujb_workload_home_capture LETTER -- emit the absolute workload home path
258	# on the remote node for the given shell-letter. _capture form per BCG line
259	# 502; returns 1 on unsupported platform or invalid letter, callers must
260	# `|| buc_die`.
261	zbujb_workload_home_capture() {
262	  zbujb_sentinel
263	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
264	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
265	  case "${z_letter}" in
266	    b)
267	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
268	        bubep_linux) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
269	        bubep_mac)   echo "/Users/${z_wlu}" ;;
270	        *)           return 1 ;;
271	      esac
272	      ;;
273	    c|w) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
274	    *)   return 1 ;;
275	  esac
276	}
277	
278	# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
279	# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
280	# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
281	# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
282	zbujb_admin_exec() {
283	  zbujb_sentinel
284	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
285	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
286	
287	  local z_remote_invoker
288	  case "${z_letter}" in
289	    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
290	    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
291	    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
292	  esac
293	
294	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
295	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
296	      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
297	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
298	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
299	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
300	      "${z_remote_invoker}"
301	}
302	
303	######################################################################
304	# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})
305	
306	# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
307	zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
308	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
309	  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST})"
310	
311	  local z_exit=0
312	  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
313	  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
314	    case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
315	      bubep_windows)
316	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BUZ_FOLIO}"
317	        ;;
318	      *)
319	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}' first."
320	        ;;
321	    esac
322	  fi
323	}
324	
325	# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
326	zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
327	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
328	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
329	  local z_wlhome
330	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
331	    || buc_die "step2: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
332	  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"
333	
334	  case "${z_letter}" in
335	    b)
336	      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
337	set -uo pipefail
338	sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
339	sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
340	SCRIPT
341	      ;;
342	    c)
343	      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
344	      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
345	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
346	set -uo pipefail
347	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
348	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
349	rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
350	SCRIPT
351	      ;;
352	    w)
353	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
354	set -uo pipefail
355	userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
356	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
357	SCRIPT
358	      ;;
359	  esac
360	}
361	
362	# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
363	zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
364	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
365	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
366	  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"
367	
368	  case "${z_letter}" in
369	    b)
370	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
371	        bubep_linux)
372	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
373	set -euo pipefail
374	sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
375	sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
376	SCRIPT
377	          ;;
378	        bubep_mac)
379	          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
380	          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
381	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
382	set -euo pipefail
383	sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
384	sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
385	SCRIPT
386	          ;;
387	      esac
388	      ;;
389	    c)
390	      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
391	      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
392	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
393	set -euo pipefail
394	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
395	mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
396	mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
397	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
398	SCRIPT
399	      ;;
400	    w)
401	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
402	set -euo pipefail
403	useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
404	passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
405	SCRIPT
406	      ;;
407	  esac
408	}
409	
410	# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
411	# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
412	zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
413	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
414	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
415	  local z_wlhome
416	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
417	    || buc_die "step4: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
418	  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"
419	
420	  local z_command_directive
421	  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for_capture "${z_letter}") \
422	    || buc_die "step4: bujb_command_for_capture failed for letter='${z_letter}'"
423	
424	  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
425	      > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK}" \
426	      2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK}" \
427	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK}"
428	  local z_pubkey
429	  z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK}")
430	  z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"
431	
432	  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"
433	
434	  # Precompute sudo prefix locally (bash parameter expansion, no $()) so the
435	  # heredoc lines don't carry inline `$([ ... ] && echo "sudo -n ")` calls.
436	  local z_sudo_prefix=""
437	  test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo_prefix="sudo -n "
438	
439	  case "${z_letter}" in
440	    b|w)
441	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
442	set -euo pipefail
443	${z_sudo_prefix}mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
444	${z_sudo_prefix}chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
445	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | ${z_sudo_prefix}tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
446	${z_sudo_prefix}chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
447	${z_sudo_prefix}chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
448	SCRIPT
449	      ;;
450	    c)
451	      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
452	      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
453	      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
454	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
455	set -euo pipefail
456	mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
457	chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
458	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
459	chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
460	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
461	SCRIPT
462	      ;;
463	  esac
464	}
465	
466	# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
467	# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
468	zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
469	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
470	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
471	  local z_wlhome
472	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
473	    || buc_die "step5: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
474	  local z_keypath
475	  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture "${z_letter}") \
476	    || buc_die "step5: bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture failed for letter='${z_letter}'"
477	  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
478	  local z_target_dir="${z_target%/*}"  # parameter expansion replaces dirname
479	  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"
480	
481	  openssl enc -base64 -A < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
482	      > "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}" \
483	      2> "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}" \
484	    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}"
485	  local z_key_b64
486	  z_key_b64=$(<"${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}")
487	  z_key_b64="${z_key_b64//$'\n'/}"
488	
489	  local z_sudo_prefix=""
490	  test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo_prefix="sudo -n "
491	
492	  case "${z_letter}" in
493	    b|w)
494	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
495	set -euo pipefail
496	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
497	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
498	echo '${z_key_b64}' | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "\${ztmp}"
499	${z_sudo_prefix}mkdir -p   '${z_target_dir}'
500	${z_sudo_prefix}install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
501	SCRIPT
502	      ;;
503	    c)
504	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
505	set -euo pipefail
506	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
507	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
508	echo '${z_key_b64}' | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "\${ztmp}"
509	mkdir -p   '${z_target_dir}'
510	cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
511	chmod 600  '${z_target}'
512	chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
513	SCRIPT
514	      ;;
515	  esac
516	}
517	
518	# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
519	zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
520	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
521	  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"
522	
523	  local z_exit=0
524	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
525	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
526	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
527	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
528	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
529	      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
530	      true                                        \
531	    || z_exit=$?
532	
533	  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
534	    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
535	}
536	
537	######################################################################
538	# Public: Garrison ceremony
539	
540	# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
541	# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
542	bujb_garrison() {
543	  zbujb_sentinel
544	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
545	    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
546	
547	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
548	  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"
549	
550	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"
551	
552	  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
553	  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
554	  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
555	  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
556	  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
557	  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"
558	
559	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
560	}
561	
562	######################################################################
563	# Internal: Fenestrate helpers (BUSJPF — Windows OpenSSH only)
564	#
565	# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
566	# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
567	# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
568	# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
569	# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
570	# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
571	# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.
572	
573	zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
574	  zbujb_sentinel
575	  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
576	    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
577	}
578	
579	# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
580	# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
581	# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
582	# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
583	# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
584	# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
585	zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback() {
586	  zbujb_sentinel
587	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
588	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
589	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
590	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"
591	
592	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
593	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
594	      -o BatchMode=no                                      \
595	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password       \
596	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
597	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
598	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
599	      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
600	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
601	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
602	}
603	
604	# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
605	# Same as above but BatchMode=yes (no password fallback). Used for phase 2.
606	zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly() {
607	  zbujb_sentinel
608	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
609	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
610	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stdout_file required"
611	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stderr_file required"
612	
613	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
614	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
615	      -o BatchMode=yes                                     \
616	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey                \
617	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
618	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
619	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
620	      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
621	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
622	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
623	}
624	
625	# zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
626	# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
627	# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
628	# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
629	# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
630	zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives() {
631	  zbujb_sentinel
632	  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
633	  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file required"
634	  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"
635	
636	  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
637	  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
638	  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"
639	
640	  local z_directives_roll=()
641	  local z_pair=""
642	  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
643	    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
644	    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
645	  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
646	
647	  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
648	  local z_line=""
649	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
650	    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
651	  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"
652	
653	  local z_directive=""
654	  local z_expected=""
655	  local z_actual=""
656	  local z_after_directive=""
657	  local z_i=0
658	  local z_j=0
659	  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
660	    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
661	    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
662	    z_actual=""
663	
664	    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
665	      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
666	        "${z_directive} "*)
667	          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
668	          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
669	          break
670	          ;;
671	      esac
672	    done
673	
674	    test -n "${z_actual}" \
675	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
676	    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
677	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
678	  done
679	}
680	
681	######################################################################
682	# Internal: Fenestrate phases
683	
684	# zbujb_fenestrate_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
685	# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
686	# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
687	# expected — exit code ignored).
688	zbujb_fenestrate_phase1() {
689	  zbujb_sentinel
690	
691	  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" \
692	      > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}" \
693	      2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}" \
694	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}"
695	  local z_pubkey
696	  z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}")
697	  z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"
698	
699	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk A: pubkey + icacls + sshd_config + sshd -t + emit (operator may be prompted for admin password on first run)"
700	
701	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
702	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"      \
703	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"      \
704	    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"
705	\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
706	
707	\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
708	\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
709	\$sshConfig    = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config"
710	
711	# Idempotent admin pubkey install
712	if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
713	  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
714	}
715	\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
716	if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
717	  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
718	}
719	
720	# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
721	icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant 'SYSTEM:F' /grant 'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
722	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
723	
724	# Idempotent sshd_config harden — replace the first matching line for
725	# each directive (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
726	# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
727	\$directives = [ordered]@{
728	  'PubkeyAuthentication'   = 'yes'
729	  'PasswordAuthentication' = 'no'
730	  'PermitEmptyPasswords'   = 'no'
731	}
732	
733	\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
734	\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
735	\$seen  = @{}
736	foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
737	  \$matched = \$false
738	  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
739	    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
740	      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
741	        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
742	        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
743	      }
744	      \$matched = \$true
745	      break
746	    }
747	  }
748	  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
749	}
750	foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
751	  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
752	}
753	Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII
754	
755	# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op; aborts before restart on bad config
756	& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
757	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
758	
759	# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (last op so output is clean)
760	Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
761	PS1
762	
763	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
764	  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"
765	
766	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk B: Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — exit code ignored)"
767	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
768	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT}"     \
769	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR}"     \
770	    <<'PS1' || true
771	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
772	Restart-Service sshd
773	PS1
774	}
775	
776	# zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
777	# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
778	zbujb_fenestrate_phase2() {
779	  zbujb_sentinel
780	
781	  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"
782	
783	  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
784	  sleep 3
785	
786	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly                 \
787	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"       \
788	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"       \
789	    <<'PS1' || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"
790	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
791	Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config" -Raw
792	PS1
793	
794	  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"
795	}
796	
797	######################################################################
798	# Public: Fenestrate ceremony
799	
800	# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
801	# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
802	bujb_fenestrate() {
803	  zbujb_sentinel
804	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
805	    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
806	
807	  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform
808	
809	  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"
810	
811	  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
812	  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2
813	
814	  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
815	}
816	
817	# eof
818
